title: Bulk importer
subtitle: Onboard your existing estate from a CSV or JSON file — preview every row before anything is written.

gate.title: Importer is admin-only
gate.description: Bulk import needs the import:run permission, granted to administrators by default. Ask an administrator if you need to onboard an estate.

upload.title: Choose a file
upload.description: Asset import is available now. Users, consumables and history import are coming later.
upload.fileHint: Export your spreadsheet as CSV (UTF-8) or provide a JSON array. .xlsx is not accepted — export it to CSV first.
upload.parsingHint: Your file is being parsed in the background. This usually takes a few seconds.

summary.title: Parsed file
summary.description: Confirm the detected shape before mapping the columns.
summary.noHeaders: No columns were detected in this file.
summary.noRows: No records were found in this file. Check the file and upload again.

mapping.title: Map your columns
mapping.description: Each card below is a column from your file, with example values. For every column, pick the lazyit field it fills, create a custom field saved to the asset's details, or ignore it. You need a column mapped to Name and one to Status.
mapping.piiNote: These cards show real values from your file, including any employee data (names, emails). Nothing is saved until you commit.
mapping.noSamples: This column is empty in the sampled rows.
mapping.customNote: Saved to the asset's details (specs) under this name. Only rows with a value get it.
mapping.customNameMissing: Name every custom field, or change it back to Ignore.
mapping.customNameDuplicate: “{name}” is already used by another custom field. Use a unique name.
mapping.customNameReserved: “{name}” is a reserved field name. Pick a different name or map this column to that field directly.
mapping.duplicateTarget: Two columns map to “{field}”. Only one can — change this one or the other.
mapping.personIdentityRequired: You started mapping a person. Map Email, Employee no. or Username too so we can identify who the asset belongs to.
mapping.personNameRequired: Map the person's name ("Assigned to") so the assigned user can be created.
mapping.statusInColumnNote: Map each status value in this column to a lazyit status. Common synonyms are filled in for you.
mapping.statusNoValues: No status values were found in the sampled rows.

modelConfig.title: Model brand and category
modelConfig.description: Map a column to Manufacturer or Category above to set them per row, or pin a value here to apply it to every model the import creates.

preview.title: Dry-run preview
preview.description: Nothing has been written. Review the per-row outcomes and resolve any conflicts below.
preview.allInvalid: No rows are valid. Go back and review the mapping or the file.
preview.tagCollisionDescription: {count, plural, one {# row carries an asset tag that already exists on a live asset} other {# rows carry an asset tag that already exists on a live asset}}. Resolve the duplicate tag in your file before committing those rows.

conflicts.title: Resolve conflicts
conflicts.description: For each referenced record we found below, choose how to resolve it. We never pick for you when more than one match exists.
conflicts.none: No conflicts found. Continue to commit.
conflicts.ambiguous: Multiple matches — you must choose one.
conflicts.createNote: A new record is created on commit with phase-1 defaults you can edit afterward.
conflicts.skipNote: Rows referencing this value are imported without the link.
conflicts.unresolved: Resolve every conflict before continuing: {count} left.

commit.runningHint: This runs in the background and may take a moment for large files. You can keep this page open.
commit.partialNote: Some rows could not be imported. Successful rows are kept — fix the failures below and re-run if needed.

errors.upload: We could not start the import. Check the file type and size, then try again.
errors.tooLarge: That file is too large to import. Split it or reduce its size and try again.
errors.forbidden: You do not have permission to run imports.
errors.notFound: This import session no longer exists. It may have expired (sessions last 24 hours). Start a new import.
errors.conflict: This import can't continue from its current step. Start over.
errors.parse: We couldn't parse this file. Make sure it's valid CSV (UTF-8) or a JSON array, then upload again.
errors.mapping: We couldn't save the mapping. Review the required fields and try again.
errors.dryRun: The dry-run failed. Go back and review the mapping, then try again.
errors.plan: We couldn't save the resolution plan. Try again.
errors.commit: The commit could not be started. You may be missing a write permission for a record the plan creates.
errors.result: We couldn't load the import result. Refresh to retry.
errors.expired: This import session expired. Start a new import.
